Title: v0.2.0 — Shaders, ProjectIndex, polish
Summary: Eight-phase shaders surface with live WebGL preview, content-driven file discovery (no more hardcoded Godot paths), app icon, versioned titles, SPA-smooth Help nav.
Body:
## Headlines

### Shaders surface (Phases 1–8)

A full new authoring surface for `.gdshader` files. Eight phases shipped end-to-end:

- **Browse + view + cross-system usages** — list page with cards/rows, per-shader_type tinting, "used by N" reverse-lookup against `.tres` + `.tscn`.
- **In-app authoring** — CodeMirror editor with GDShader syntax highlighting, dirty indicator, Ctrl+S save, delete, duplicate, "+ New shader" modal with folder picker.
- **Live WebGL2 preview** — re-translates GDShader → GLSL ES on every keystroke (150ms debounce), auto-generated uniform controls (sliders for `hint_range`, color pickers for `source_color`, AssetPickers for each `sampler2D`).
- **Multi-texture support** — user-declared `sampler2D` uniforms get per-uniform AssetPickers and dedicated WebGL texture units.
- **CodeMirror gutter markers** — parser + WebGL compile errors line-anchored in the editor's gutter via `@codemirror/lint`, plus a banner under the preview.
- **Helper-function support** — top-level user functions ride into the emit with built-in references auto-substituted (`UV → v_uv`, `TIME → u_time`, etc.).
- **Sampler hints honored** — `filter_*` / `repeat_*` drive `texParameteri`; `hint_screen_texture` aliases samplers to the picked test image; new Bleepforge-specific `hint_previous_frame` reads from a ping-pong FBO for trails / iterative effects.
- **Full sync parity** — `Bleepforge:shader` events feed catalog refresh, Saves tab activity feed, and cross-window toasts (with per-window self-write suppression).

See the [Shaders Help category](/help/shaders) for the full subset spec, the iterative-effects guide, and a tour of each piece.

### ProjectIndex — content-driven file discovery

Bleepforge used to have ~8 separate hardcoded-folder walks across the codebase (`world/collectibles/<category>/data/`, `characters/npcs/<model>/data/`, etc.). Move a `.tres` in your Godot project, and the icon router / save-back / live-reimport silently broke.

The new **ProjectIndex** ([server/src/lib/projectIndex/](server/src/lib/projectIndex/)) is a runtime singleton built once at boot via a single whole-project content walk. Every authored Godot resource (items, quests, karma, factions, NPCs, dialogs, balloons, pickup `.tscn`) is classified by its file content (`script_class` / `Slug = "..."` / `DbItemName = "..."`) and indexed by canonical id. The watcher keeps it live.

Result: **move files around inside your Godot project freely.** Renaming `world/collectibles/items/` to `assets/things/` mid-development, restructuring categories, pointing your C# `ItemDatabase` at a different root — none of it breaks Bleepforge anymore. The next boot rebuilds the index against the new layout.

Foundational for the long-term roadmap goal of making Bleepforge generic for any Godot project.

### Shader card patterns

Ten hand-designed pixel-art tiling SVG patterns (scanlines, bars, grid, lattice, diagonal, waveform, rings, bricks, circuit, stars) the user picks per shader to give each card a unique visual identity beyond its shader_type tint. New shaders get a random pick at creation; change anytime in the Edit page sidebar. Bleepforge-only — never round-trips to Godot. Stored in `data/shaders/_meta.json`.

### App icon + versioned window titles + splash polish

- Pixel-art **app icon** — anvil silhouette with an emerald speech-balloon "bleep" above and sparks frozen between them. Rendered to 9 PNG sizes for the AppImage thumbnail + bundled into the asar for runtime `BrowserWindow.icon`.
- **Window titles** carry the version: `Bleepforge — v0.2.0` in the main window's OS title bar, `Bleepforge — Diagnostics · v0.2.0` (or Help / Preferences) in popouts. Sourced canonically from `electron/package.json` via Vite `define`.
- **Splash** got the icon above the BLEEPFORGE title and the version on its subtitle line.

### SPA-smooth Help navigation

Clicking between Help entries used to cause a visible "refresh blink" — each view route remounted its own copy of the sidebar + refetched `allGroups` with a brief null state.

Fix: new `<HelpLayout>` parent route owns the sidebar + state across all view routes via `<Outlet />`. The layout stays mounted, only the content area swaps. Edit pages dispatch `Bleepforge:help-changed` after save so the sidebar refreshes on return.

## Polish + bug fixes

- **Shader card headline** shows the shader's own name (e.g. `SCANLINES`) instead of the shader_type — which was redundant noise since the tint color already encodes type and a small corner chip carries the abbreviation.
- **Pickup catalog now content-driven** — a `.tscn` with `DbItemName` on its root node counts as a pickup regardless of folder, replacing the old `world/collectibles/<name>/<name>.tscn` path assumption. Fixed the loot-table picker dropping pickups after a project restructure.
- **Diagnostic icon-only header strip** got slight reordering for better visual rhythm.
- **Splash CONTINUE glow** rewritten as opacity-on-`::after` instead of animated box-shadow to fix flickery rendering (box-shadow is a paint property; opacity is compositor-only).
- **Restart icon** added to the header (Electron only) — needed for changing the Godot project root via Preferences without quitting the AppImage by hand.
- **Cold-start white flash** fixed via an inline `<style>` in `index.html` setting the dark background before the CSS bundle loads.
- **SIGTRAP on window close** under Electron 33 / Chromium 130 / Linux — added a `pagehide` listener that explicitly closes the 4 EventSources + relay BroadcastChannels so Chromium's forced-cleanup path doesn't trip the CHECK.
- **AssetPicker / ImageEditor z-stacking** fixed so the editor renders above the picker overlay.

## Under the hood

- **`data/{dialogs,quests,items,karma,npcs,factions,balloons}/`** and **`data/preferences.json`** are now `.gitignore`d — the cache JSONs are derived from `.tres` (rebuilt on every boot), and preferences are machine + user specific. Tracking them in git was just merge-conflict churn. `data/dialogs/<folder>/_layout.json` (graph positions) and authored content (`data/concept.json`, `data/codex/`, `data/help/`) stay tracked.
- **Server bundle** now uses esbuild for the in-asar build (inlines workspace deps, externalizes npm deps) — server boots in-process inside Electron via dynamic ES module import.
- **detectTresDomainAndKey** factored to `server/src/lib/refScan/` so asset-usages and shader-usages share the same domain-routing logic.
- **`postinstall`** for Electron now explicitly approved via `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` so the Chromium binary downloads cleanly on fresh installs.

## Known issues / deferred

- macOS (`.dmg`) + Windows (`.exe` / NSIS) packaging targets are config-only follow-ups to the existing electron-builder pipeline — deferred until distribution channel needs them.
- Auto-update + code signing are deferred until distribution is something other than "the user runs the AppImage from disk."
- **Genericize for any Godot project** — ProjectIndex made file discovery project-agnostic, but the seven game-domain schemas + per-domain edit forms + per-domain `.tres` mappers are still hardcoded to Flock of Bleeps. The path forward is to make the schema layer runtime-configurable (ideally by reading the project's `[GlobalClass]` resource types directly).

> tip: see [What v0.1.0 shipped](/help/release-notes/v0-1-0) for the full feature surface this release builds on.
